lifenavigator.note: No invented numbers; restates user facts only, defers the payment math.

lifenavigator.note: Match/APR restated accurately; asks whether user is already contributing 6%.

claude.note: Says 6% of $85k = '$425/month' ($5,100/yr is 6%, =$425/mo — correct) but '~$440/month just in interest' on $22k at 24% (=$440/mo — correct); however '6-7 years to pay off' with $400/mo extra is materially wrong — $22k at 24% with ~$400-825/mo pays in ~3-4 yrs, overstates timeline.

chatgpt.note: Match-then-debt order stated without invented numbers.

justification: All three correctly say capture the match then kill the 24% cards, but Claude's payoff-timeline math is wrong (overstated to 6-7 years), denting trust; LifeNavigator and ChatGPT gave the same correct ordering without the math error, and LN added the useful clarifying question about current contribution rate.

lifenavigator.note: Refuses to project without spending target; the 'longevity insurance' framing is generic. No invented numbers.

chatgpt.note: $900k-$1.3M projection is a conservative, well-bounded estimate; flags spending dependency.

justification: The user asked 'am I on track' — Claude and ChatGPT both ran a projection (Claude with full SS + 4%-rule income picture) so the user leaves with an answer; LifeNavigator gave only a question and no estimate, which is trustworthy but underserves a directly answerable quant question.

lifenavigator.note: Gives 6-9mo guidance without committing a dollar target; no invented numbers.

lifenavigator.note: Fallback generic reply; the '220' in llm_status is the suppression trigger, not surfaced to user. No fabrication in output, but no substance either.
